claim_bullets.bullet_text: Paine proposes a continental charter for the colonies, similar to England's Magna Charta (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.16-22).
He argues the law should be the ruler in America, not a monarch (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.16-22).
Paine warns that not forming a constitution now may lead to tyranny (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.16-22).
He suggests that a social compact would dissolve if the government does not act (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.16-22).
Paine believes it is unwise to spend millions for the sake of repealing a few acts (raw/1776ThomasPaine.pdf p.16-22).

source_record_text: Thomas Paine's 'Document' section from '1776', pages 16-22, discusses forming a continental charter and the necessity of a constitution.
